user_submission_summary: User identified 2-on-1, wanted to challenge carrier early, noted middle drive but ranked shot as main threat; did not mention cross-crease as primary failure mode.

visual_grounding.note: Four cues cited; carrier and middle drive accurate; handedness not addressed

scan_completeness.note: Carrier and middle drive covered; weak-side and backchecker timing thin

read_quality.note: Challenge read contradicts open middle lane in frame

threat_identification.note: Named shot but not cross-crease pass as beat-you play

metacognition.note: Stated unsure on handedness — good honesty

what_you_got_right: Correctly classified as odd-man rush with puck on right wing
Identified second attacker in middle lane with stick ready
Noted you were at top of crease — appropriate starting depth for delay
Flagged handedness as unsure instead of guessing

gaps.title: Challenge vs. delay
gaps.user_said: I would challenge the carrier at the circle to cut the angle.
gaps.litigation: You said challenge — that cuts the shot angle when the pass is taken away. In this frame the middle drive is open and the backchecker is late; challenging sells out your lateral push. Playbook §2.1: default is hold depth and delay until commitment.

gaps.title: Beat-you threat
gaps.user_said: If I'm wrong, they score on the shot.
gaps.litigation: The shot is live, but the highest-percentage beat-you play on a 2-on-1 with middle drive is cross-crease pass when you over-commit. Naming only the shot understates the pass threat.

read_comparison.coach_read_summary: Hold depth at top of crease, delay, respect cross-crease
read_comparison.why: Open middle lane and late backchecker favor delay over challenge in this frame

false_musts: User treated shot as the must-happen beat-you play; cross-crease pass is the must for beating a delay read here
missing_branches: Cross-crease pass branch not listed in could-happen table
No doesn't-need section to deprioritize weak-side wrap

missed_threats: Cross-crease pass to middle drive before backchecker arrives
Carrier pass fake vs. shot at same wind-up
study_points: §14 Assumption Framework — must happen for cross-crease vs shot
§13 Relationship Graph — middle drive enables pass, contradicts challenge

next_step.message: Retry teach-back focusing on pass threat and beat-you play, or say 'coach reveal' for the full breakdown with outcome paths.
